title: Ensure that encryption providers are appropriately configured

input_row.title: Ensure that encryption providers are appropriately configured
input_row.description: Where etcd encryption is used, appropriate providers should be configured.
input_row.rationale: Where etcd encryption is used, it is important to ensure that the appropriate set of encryption providers is used. Currently, the aescbc, kms, and secretbox are likely to be appropriate options. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Get the EncryptionConfig file set for --encryption-provider-config argument. Verify that aescbc, kms, or secretbox is set as the encryption provider for all the desired resources.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and configure a EncryptionConfig file. In this file, choose aescbc, kms, or secretbox as the encryption provider. Default Value: By default, no encryption provider is set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/encrypt-data/ 2. https://acotten.com/post/kube17-security 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kube- apiserver/ 4. https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/issues/92 5. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/encrypt-data/#providers

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The configuration of encryption providers for etcd (as specified in the --encryption-provider-config argument to the kube-apiserver) is not exposed via the Kubernetes API or kubectl. The EncryptionConfig file is a file on disk, and its contents (the specific providers used: aescbc, kms, secretbox) are not queryable via any Kubernetes API or resource. Verification requires access to the control plane host filesystem or process arguments, and inspection of the referenced file.
- The audit procedure in the CIS Benchmark instructs to run OS-level commands (ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver) and to review the contents of a file on disk, which cannot be performed via Kubernetes APIs or kubectl.
- Therefore, this check requires manual review.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. On the control plane node, run the following command to identify the kube-apiserver process and locate the --encryption-provider-config argument:
   ```
   ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver
   ```
2. Note the path specified for the --encryption-provider-config flag.
3. Open the referenced EncryptionConfig file for review:
   ```
   cat /path/to/encryption-provider-config.yaml
   ```
4. Examine the providers listed for each resource. Ensure that only aescbc, kms, or secretbox are used as encryption providers for all desired resources (e.g., secrets, configmaps).
5. Confirm that no insecure or deprecated providers (such as identity or aesgcm) are used for resource encryption.
6. Document compliance if only aescbc, kms, or secretbox are present as providers for all relevant resources. If other providers are present, remediation is required.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.11 PDF
